flashcards.back: User Datagram Protocol. It is a core Transport Layer protocol in the Internet Protocol Suite.

flashcards.front: What layer of the OSI model does UDP operate at?
flashcards.back: UDP operates at the Transport Layer (Layer 4) of the OSI model.
UDP is connectionless. It does not establish a dedicated end-to-end connection before transmitting data.
No, UDP provides unreliable (best-effort) delivery. There is no acknowledgment, sequencing, or retransmission of lost packets.

flashcards.front: What is the size of the UDP header?
flashcards.back: The UDP header is 8 bytes (64 bits) fixed size. It contains Source Port (2 bytes), Destination Port (2 bytes), Length (2 bytes), and Checksum (2 bytes).

flashcards.front: What are the fields in a UDP header?
flashcards.back: The four fields are: Source Port (16 bits), Destination Port (16 bits), Length (16 bits), and Checksum (16 bits).

flashcards.front: What is the maximum size of a UDP datagram?
flashcards.back: The maximum UDP datagram size is 65,535 bytes (64 KB), including the 8-byte header. This is the maximum payload of the underlying IP packet.
No, UDP does not provide flow control. There is no mechanism to prevent the sender from overwhelming the receiver.
No, UDP does not provide congestion control. It does not throttle sending based on network conditions.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the UDP Checksum?
flashcards.back: The UDP Checksum provides error detection for the UDP header and data. It can optionally verify the pseudo-header for additional reliability.

flashcards.front: Is the UDP Checksum mandatory in IPv4?
flashcards.back: In IPv4, the UDP Checksum is optional (set to 0 means no checksum). In IPv6, the UDP Checksum is mandatory.
No, UDP does not provide sequencing. Packets may arrive out of order or be duplicated.

flashcards.front: Name three applications that use UDP.
flashcards.back: DNS (Domain Name System), DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol), VoIP (Voice over IP), and video streaming applications use UDP.

flashcards.front: What is a well-known UDP port number for DNS?
flashcards.back: DNS uses port 53. DNS queries typically use UDP port 53, though TCP can also be used for zone transfers.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of UDP over TCP?
flashcards.back: UDP has minimal overhead (8-byte header vs 20-byte TCP header), providing lower latency and faster transmission, ideal for real-time applications.
